excerpt: Every SabioTrade rule explained in plain language — drawdown limits, profit targets, trading restrictions, and payout terms.
content:
SabioTrade Rules Overview

SabioTrade is a proprietary trading firm founded in 2023 that specializes in simplified 1-step evaluation—eliminating the traditional 2-phase challenge model. Traders can access funded accounts up to $650,000 across major asset classes: forex, stocks, indices, commodities, crypto, and ETFs. The firm is operated by CODEVIL IT ENGINEERING LIMITED, headquartered in Dublin, Ireland, and maintains a 3.8/5 star rating.

The key differentiator: you reach a funded account faster without progressing through multiple evaluation phases. However, note that futures trading is not supported—a significant limitation for certain trading strategies.

Account Types and Pricing

SabioTrade offers five subscription tiers. Your selected tier determines access to features (mobile app, performance analytics, risk management tools, news feed, education) and funding levels. All tiers require an upfront evaluation/assessment fee, which is 100% refunded after your first profitable payout.

Account Tier Monthly Subscription Typical Fit
Essential $119/month Beginner traders, first-time evaluations
Plus $289/month Intermediate traders with basic analytics needs
Advanced $479/month Experienced traders, advanced risk tools
Ultimate $939/month Professional traders, premium support
Prime $2,989/month Institutional/serious full-time traders

Note: SabioTrade offers no free tier. The assessment fee (amount not publicly disclosed) applies to all tiers but is refunded after your first profitable payout.

Profit Targets

SabioTrade's exact profit target percentages are not detailed in public documentation. However, the firm's 1-step evaluation model means you face a single profit target rather than progressing through Phase 1 and Phase 2 challenges as with competitors.

  • Profit targets are typically calculated as a percentage of your funded account balance
  • The 1-step structure suggests a single milestone to reach funded status, not multiple phases
  • Once you meet the profit target and maintain position compliance, you qualify for payouts

Required: Before trading, request SabioTrade's official profit target percentage for your selected account tier. This is critical for evaluating difficulty and break-even timelines.

Drawdown Rules

SabioTrade's specific drawdown limits (daily maximum, monthly/trailing maximum, and reset mechanics) are not disclosed in public marketing materials. Traders must verify these directly with the firm.

Industry standard prop firm drawdown structures typically include:

  • Daily Drawdown Limit: Usually 5–10% of account balance; breaching ends your trading day
  • Maximum Drawdown: Usually 10–20% of account balance; exceeding this terminates your evaluation or funded account
  • Calculation Method: Trailing drawdown (from highest equity point) or fixed drawdown (from account start)
  • Reset Conditions: Daily limits reset at market open; maximum drawdown typically persists throughout the evaluation period

Action Required: Before funding an account, obtain SabioTrade's Risk Management Policy detailing exact daily and maximum drawdown percentages, calculation method, and account termination triggers.

Trading Restrictions

SabioTrade enforces the following confirmed restrictions:

  • Futures Trading: NOT supported. Available instruments are limited to forex, stocks, indices, commodities, crypto, and ETFs only
  • Leverage Limits: Not publicly specified—confirm maximum leverage for your asset class with the firm
  • Lot Size / Position Limits: Not publicly specified—request position sizing limits for your account tier
  • News Trading: Policy not disclosed; many prop firms restrict trading during major economic data releases—verify with SabioTrade
  • Expert Advisors & Automated Trading: Confirm whether EAs, trading bots, and algorithmic strategies are permitted
  • Copy Trading / Signal Following: Confirm whether copying other traders or following third-party signals is allowed
  • Weekend / Holiday Holdings: Confirm whether positions held over weekends incur additional margin or are restricted

Next Step: Request the full Terms of Service and Risk Management Policy to understand all trading restrictions for your chosen tier.

Profit Split and Payouts

Profit Share:

  • 90% profit split on funded accounts $100,000 and above (SabioTrade takes 10%)
  • Smaller account tiers (Essential–Advanced): confirm exact profit split percentage with the firm
  • Profit share is calculated on net gains only (losses reduce your payout)

Payout Schedule & Processing:

  • Payout Frequency: Weekly (not monthly like most competitors)
  • Processing Time: Funds typically arrive within 24 hours of payout processing
  • Minimum Withdrawal Amount: Not publicly disclosed—confirm with the firm
  • Payment Methods: Not specified—verify available withdrawal methods for your region (bank transfer, wire, crypto, etc.)

Assessment Fee Refund: Your initial evaluation fee is 100% refunded after your first profitable payout. This is relatively generous compared to competitors who offer 50–0% refunds or none at all.

⚠️ Known Issue: Some traders have reported payout disputes on Trustpilot, with delayed or contested withdrawals. Customer support has been reported as slow to resolve payment issues. Before your first payout, understand SabioTrade's dispute resolution process.

Scaling Plan

Limitation: SabioTrade does not offer scaling plans. This is a major difference from competitors.

  • Your funded account size is fixed based on your subscription tier
  • Account size does not automatically increase based on trading performance or profitability
  • If you want a larger funded account, you must manually upgrade to a higher subscription tier or open a separate evaluation
  • Maximum available account size is $650,000 (assuming this is available in the Prime tier; confirm specific sizes per tier)

For traders planning multi-year growth strategies, the absence of scaling is a significant constraint. You won't see organic account expansion through the firm's standard progression model.

SabioTrade Rules vs Competitors

How SabioTrade compares on key trading rules and features versus typical industry competitors:

Feature SabioTrade Typical Competitor A Typical Competitor B
Evaluation Model 1-Step (Simplified) 2-Step (Phase 1 → Phase 2) 2–3 Step (multi-phase)
Max Account Size $650,000 $500,000 $1,000,000+
Profit Split (Premium Tier) 90% 80–85% 70–75%
Payout Frequency Weekly Monthly Bi-weekly to Monthly
Payout Speed ~24 hours 3–7 business days 2–5 business days
Assessment Fee Refund 100% after first profit 50% conditional 0% (non-refundable)
Scaling Plans None Yes (progressive tiers) Yes (aggressive scaling)
Futures Support No Yes Yes

SabioTrade Wins:

  • Fastest route to funded account (1-step vs 2–3 steps)
  • Most frequent payouts (weekly vs monthly)
  • Fastest payout speed (24 hours vs 3–7 days)
  • Industry-leading assessment fee refund (100% after first profit)
  • Competitive 90% profit split on larger accounts

Where Competitors Win Against SabioTrade:

  • Scaling: Competitors offer progressive account increases; SabioTrade does not
  • Maximum Account Size: Competitors often offer $1M+ funded accounts; SabioTrade maxes at $650K
  • Futures: Competitors support futures; SabioTrade restricts you to forex, stocks, indices, commodities, crypto, ETFs
  • Profit Tiers: Competitors typically offer better splits across all tier levels; SabioTrade's top 90% split applies only to $100K+ accounts

Red Flags and Fine Print

  • Payout Disputes: According to Trustpilot reviews, some traders have experienced delays or disputes in receiving payouts despite the advertised 24-hour promise. Always confirm payout eligibility criteria before submitting withdrawal requests.
  • Slow Customer Support on Payment Issues: Multiple reports indicate that resolving payment-related inquiries takes longer than expected. If a payout is delayed or disputed, expect extended wait times for resolution.
  • No Scaling = Growth Ceiling: Unlike competitors offering progressive account increases ($50K → $100K → $500K → $1M), SabioTrade's fixed account sizes mean you cannot grow your funded balance through the firm itself. This severely limits long-term compounding potential.
  • Futures Trading Excluded: If your strategy relies on commodity futures, equity indices futures, or crypto futures, SabioTrade is not an option. This is a hard restriction, not a policy that may change.
  • Subscription Cost Discipline: The higher subscription tiers ($479–$2,989/month) add significant cost beyond the assessment fee. Calculate your monthly break-even point: if you're trading a $100K account with a 90% split, you need consistent profits to cover subscription costs and generate net gains.
  • Limited Funded Sizes Per Tier (Unconfirmed): The tool data does not specify which account sizes ($50K, $100K, $250K, $500K, $650K) are available under each subscription tier. A trader paying $119/month (Essential) may receive a $50K account, while a $2,989/month (Prime) trader receives $650K—or the firm might allocate differently. Confirm funding amounts before purchase.
  • Regulatory Clarity Needed: SabioTrade (operated by CODEVIL IT ENGINEERING LIMITED, Dublin) is not explicitly stated as FCA-regulated or registered with the Central Bank of Ireland. Verify the firm's regulatory status and license before depositing funds.
  • Terms of Service Gaps: Critical rules including exact profit targets, daily/maximum drawdown percentages, leverage limits, and position sizing are not publicly disclosed. Request a full copy of the Terms of Service and Risk Management Policy before committing.
  • Young Firm (Founded 2023): SabioTrade is relatively new to the market. Fewer long-term trader reviews means higher uncertainty about account stability and payout consistency over time. Monitor Trustpilot regularly for emerging patterns.

Overall Assessment: SabioTrade is best suited for beginner-to-intermediate traders prioritizing speed (1-step evaluation, weekly payouts) over account scaling and instrument diversity. The generous profit split and fast payouts are attractive, but payout disputes and lack of scaling are deal-breakers for serious, long-term prop traders. Review independent Trustpilot reviews before opening an account, and always request full terms documentation before funding.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
